internal.h(3) Library Functions Manual internal.h(3)

NAME

internal.h - Internal declarations for builder implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct option_builder_handler_t
Type handler for builder operations.

Macros


#define INITIAL_DESCRIPTOR_CAPACITY 32
#define INITIAL_DEPENDENCY_CAPACITY 16
#define INITIAL_POSITIONAL_ARG_CAPACITY 8
#define INITIAL_OWNED_STRINGS_CAPACITY 32
#define NUM_OPTION_TYPES 6

Functions


const char * get_option_help_placeholder_str (const option_descriptor_t *desc)
Get help placeholder string for an option type. int format_option_default_value_str (const option_descriptor_t *desc, char *buf, size_t bufsize)
Format option default value as a string. bool option_applies_to_mode (const option_descriptor_t *desc, asciichat_mode_t mode, bool for_binary_help)
Check if an option applies to a specific mode using bitmask. void ensure_descriptor_capacity (options_builder_t *builder)
Grow descriptor array if needed. void ensure_dependency_capacity (options_builder_t *builder)
Grow dependency array if needed. void ensure_positional_arg_capacity (options_builder_t *builder)
Grow positional arg array if needed. void ensure_owned_strings_capacity (options_builder_t *builder)
const option_descriptor_t * find_option (const options_config_t *config, const char *long_name)
Find option descriptor by long name. bool is_option_set (const options_config_t *config, const void *options_struct, const char *option_name)
Check if an option is set (has non-default value).

Variables


const option_builder_handler_t g_builder_handlers []

Detailed Description

Internal declarations for builder implementation.

Shared structures and forward declarations used across builder modules.

void ensure_dependency_capacity (options_builder_t * builder)

Grow dependency array if needed.

Definition at line 224 of file builder.c.

224                                                             {
225   if (builder->num_dependencies >= builder->dependency_capacity) {
226     size_t new_capacity = builder->dependency_capacity * 2;
227     option_dependency_t *new_dependencies =
228         SAFE_REALLOC(builder->dependencies, new_capacity * sizeof(option_dependency_t), option_dependency_t *);
229     if (!new_dependencies) {
230       log_fatal("Failed to reallocate dependencies array");
231       return;
232     }
233     builder->dependencies = new_dependencies;
234     builder->dependency_capacity = new_capacity;
235   }
236 }

    options_builder_t::num_dependencies, and SAFE_REALLOC.

void
  ensure_positional_arg_capacity (options_builder_t * builder)


Grow positional arg array if needed.


Definition at line 241 of file builder.c.


241                                                                 {
242   if (builder->num_positional_args >= builder->positional_arg_capacity) {
243     size_t new_capacity = builder->positional_arg_capacity * 2;
244     if (new_capacity == 0)
245       new_capacity = INITIAL_POSITIONAL_ARG_CAPACITY;
246 
247     positional_arg_descriptor_t *new_positional = SAFE_REALLOC(
248         builder->positional_args, new_capacity * sizeof(positional_arg_descriptor_t), positional_arg_descriptor_t *);
249     if (!new_positional) {
250       log_fatal("Failed to reallocate positional_args array");
251       return;
252     }
253     builder->positional_args = new_positional;
254     builder->positional_arg_capacity = new_capacity;
255   }
256 }

const option_descriptor_t * find_option (const
  options_config_t * config, const char * long_name)


Find option descriptor by long name.


Definition at line 338 of file builder.c.


338                                                                                               {
339   for (size_t i = 0; i < config->num_descriptors; i++) {
340     if (strcmp(config->descriptors[i].long_name, long_name) == 0) {
341       return &config->descriptors[i];
342     }
343   }
344   return NULL;
345 }

int
  format_option_default_value_str (const option_descriptor_t * desc, char
  * buf, size_t bufsize)


Format option default value as a string.


Parameters


desc Option descriptor


buf Output buffer


bufsize Size of buffer


Returns


Number of characters written to buf


Definition at line 77 of file builder.c.


77                                                                                                 {
78   if (!desc || !buf || bufsize == 0) {
79     return 0;
80   }
81 
82   // For callback options with enums, look up the enum string by matching the default value
83   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->metadata.enum_values && desc->default_value) {
84     int default_int_val = 0;
85     memcpy(&default_int_val, desc->default_value, sizeof(int));
86 
87     // Try to find matching enum value
88     if (desc->metadata.enum_integer_values) {
89       for (size_t i = 0; desc->metadata.enum_values[i] != NULL; i++) {
90         if (desc->metadata.enum_integer_values[i] == default_int_val) {
91           return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", desc->metadata.enum_values[i]);
92         }
93       }
94     } else {
95       // Fallback: assume sequential 0-based indices if integer values not provided
96       // Count enum values to check bounds
97       size_t enum_count = 0;
98       while (desc->metadata.enum_values[enum_count] != NULL) {
99         enum_count++;
100       }
101       if (default_int_val >= 0 && (size_t)default_int_val < enum_count) {
102         return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", desc->metadata.enum_values[default_int_val]);
103       }
104     }
105   }
106 
107   // For callback options storing numeric types (int/double/float), format them as numbers
108   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->default_value && desc->metadata.enum_values == NULL) {
109     // Check if this is a numeric callback by looking for numeric_range metadata
110     // Numeric callbacks have min/max range constraints set (max != 0 indicates a range was set)
111     if (desc->metadata.numeric_range.max != 0 || desc->metadata.numeric_range.min != 0) {
112       // This is a numeric callback option - extract and format the value
113       // Determine if it's int or double based on range (int max is 2147483647)
114       double default_double = 0.0;
115       if (desc->metadata.numeric_range.max <= 2147483647.0 && desc->metadata.numeric_range.min >= -2147483648.0) {
116         // Integer-ranged value (like port 1-65535) - stored as int
117         int default_int = 0;
118         memcpy(&default_int, desc->default_value, sizeof(int));
119         default_double = (double)default_int;
120       } else {
121         // Double-ranged value - stored as double
122         memcpy(&default_double, desc->default_value, sizeof(double));
123       }
124 
125       // Format with appropriate precision (remove trailing zeros for integers)
126       char formatted[32];
127       safe_snprintf(formatted, sizeof(formatted), "%.1f", default_double);
128 
129       // Remove trailing zeros after decimal point
130       char *dot = strchr(formatted, '.');
131       if (dot) {
132         char *end = formatted + strlen(formatted) - 1;
133         while (end > dot && *end == '0') {
134           end--;
135         }
136         if (*end == '.') {
137           *end = '\0';
138         } else {
139           *(end + 1) = '\0';
140         }
141       }
142 
143       return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", formatted);
144     }
145   }
146 
147   // For callback options with string defaults (no enum, no numeric range)
148   // Treat default_value as a const char* string pointer
149   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->default_value && desc->metadata.enum_values == NULL &&
150       desc->metadata.numeric_range.max == 0 && desc->metadata.numeric_range.min == 0) {
151     const char *str_default = (const char *)desc->default_value;
152     if (str_default && str_default[0] != '\0') {
153       return safe_snprintf(buf, bufsize, "%s", str_default);
154     }
155   }
156 
157   return options_format_default_value(desc->type, desc->default_value, buf, bufsize);
158 }






References option_descriptor_t::default_value,
    option_metadata_t::enum_integer_values,
    option_metadata_t::enum_values, option_metadata_t::max,
    option_descriptor_t::metadata, option_metadata_t::min,
    option_metadata_t::numeric_range, OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK,
    options_format_default_value(), safe_snprintf(), and
    option_descriptor_t::type.


Referenced by
    options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(), and
    options_config_print_usage().






const
  char * get_option_help_placeholder_str (const option_descriptor_t *
  desc)


Get help placeholder string for an option type.


Parameters


desc Option descriptor


Returns


Pointer to string literal ("INTEGER",
  "NUMBER", "STRING", "VALUE",
  "[BOOLEAN]"), custom placeholder, or empty string


Definition at line 37 of file builder.c.


37                                                                              {
38   if (!desc) {
39     return "";
40   }
41   // Check for custom placeholder first
42   if (desc->arg_placeholder != NULL) {
43     return desc->arg_placeholder;
44   }
45 
46   // For callback options, check if it's a boolean based on enum values
47   if (desc->type == OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK && desc->metadata.enum_values) {
48     // Check if enum values contain "true" and "false" (boolean-like)
49     const char *const *values = desc->metadata.enum_values;
50     bool has_true = false, has_false = false;
51 
52     for (int i = 0; values[i] != NULL; i++) {
53       if (strcmp(values[i], "true") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "yes") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "on") == 0) {
54         has_true = true;
55       }
56       if (strcmp(values[i], "false") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "no") == 0 || strcmp(values[i], "off") == 0) {
57         has_false = true;
58       }
59     }
60 
61     // If it has both true-like and false-like values, it's boolean
62     if (has_true && has_false) {
63       return "BOOLEAN";
64     }
65   }
66 
67   return options_get_type_placeholder(desc->type);
68 }






References option_descriptor_t::arg_placeholder,
    option_metadata_t::enum_values, option_descriptor_t::metadata,
    OPTION_TYPE_CALLBACK, options_get_type_placeholder(), and
    option_descriptor_t::type.


Referenced by
    options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(), and
    options_config_print_usage().






bool is_option_set (const options_config_t *
  config, const void * options_struct, const char * option_name)


Check if an option is set (has non-default value).


Definition at line 350 of file builder.c.


350                                                                                                         {
351   const option_descriptor_t *desc = find_option(config, option_name);
352   if (!desc)
353     return false;
354 
355   const char *base = (const char *)options_struct;
356   const void *field = base + desc->offset;
357 
358   // Use handler registry to check if option is set
359   if (desc->type >= 0 && desc->type < (int)(NUM_OPTION_TYPES)) {
360     if (g_builder_handlers[desc->type].is_set) {
361       return g_builder_handlers[desc->type].is_set(field, desc);
362     }
363   }
364 
365   return false;
366 }






References find_option(), g_builder_handlers,
    NUM_OPTION_TYPES, option_descriptor_t::offset, and
    option_descriptor_t::type.


Referenced by options_config_validate().






bool option_applies_to_mode (const
  option_descriptor_t * desc, asciichat_mode_t mode, bool
  for_binary_help)


Check if an option applies to a specific mode using bitmask.


Parameters


desc Option descriptor


mode Mode to check (use invalid value like -1 for binary help)


for_binary_help If true, show binary options; if false, hide them


Returns


true if option should be shown for this mode


Definition at line 171 of file builder.c.


171                                                                                                           {
172   if (!desc) {
173     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Descriptor is NULL");
174     return false;
175   }
176 
177   // When for_binary_help is true (i.e., for 'ascii-chat --help'),
178   // show options that apply to the default mode (DISCOVERY) or binary-level options only.
179   // Don't show mode-specific options for other modes (server, client, mirror, discovery-service).
180   if (for_binary_help) {
181     // Binary-level options or options that apply to discovery mode (the default)
182     option_mode_bitmask_t default_modes = OPTION_MODE_BINARY | OPTION_MODE_DISCOVERY;
183     return (desc->mode_bitmask & default_modes) != 0 && !desc->hide_from_binary_help;
184   }
185 
186   // For mode-specific help (non-discovery modes), show only options for that mode.
187   // Do not show binary options here unless it also specifically applies to the mode.
188   // The 'mode' parameter is the actual mode (e.g., MODE_CLIENT, MODE_SERVER).
189   if (mode < 0 || mode > MODE_DISCOVERY) { // Use MODE_INVALID as upper bound for valid modes
190     return false;
191   }
192   option_mode_bitmask_t mode_bit = (1 << mode);
193 
194   // Check if it's a binary option. If so, only show if it also explicitly applies to this mode.
195   if ((desc->mode_bitmask & OPTION_MODE_BINARY) && !(desc->mode_bitmask & mode_bit)) {
196     return false; // Binary options not shown in mode-specific help unless also mode-specific
197   }
198 
199   bool applies = (desc->mode_bitmask & mode_bit) != 0;
200 
201   return applies && !desc->hide_from_mode_help;
202 }






References ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    option_descriptor_t::hide_from_binary_help,
    option_descriptor_t::hide_from_mode_help,
    option_descriptor_t::mode_bitmask, MODE_DISCOVERY,
    OPTION_MODE_BINARY, OPTION_MODE_DISCOVERY, and
    SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by
    options_config_print_options_sections_with_width(), and
    options_config_print_usage().
